revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image: a crisp SNES/16-bit style pixel-art terrain tile sheet for game development.

STRICT OUTPUT GEOMETRY:
- Final image size: exactly 64x64 pixels.
- Grid: 4 rows x 4 columns.
- Each frame/tile: exactly 16x16 pixels.
- Total frames: 16.
- Every tile fills its 16x16 cell edge-to-edge.
- No visible grid lines, borders, tile IDs, labels, text, UI, watermark, or extra padding.
- Fully opaque pixels across the whole image. Do not leave transparent pixels.
- Use solid #FF00FF magenta only if any truly empty outside-art space would exist, but the tiles should fill all cells edge-to-edge so magenta should not appear between tiles.

STYLE / MATERIAL:
- Profile: snes16_platformer_v1.
- Preset: world_grass_variation_16.
- Asset kind: terrain-tile, asset type: tile.
- Bright mythical-Japan satoyama palette, but calm, quiet, low-noise, low-contrast.
- Walkable BASE grass autotile that supports cliffs, paths, water, and buildings; it must not compete visually.
- Broad muted green color drift, faint mossy texture, gentle low-frequency pixel clusters.
- Crisp low-resolution pixels, no antialiasing blur, no painterly smears.
- Consistent palette, consistent scale, top-left light source.
- Use dark one-pixel outlining only where useful and very subtle; avoid black shadows.
- Seam-friendly edges: do not create obvious repeated square stamps, checkerboard patterns, directional brush strokes, or hard rectangular bands.

STRICT 4x4 TILE LAYOUT:
Row 1, columns 1-4: four calm INTERIOR grass variations. Soft uniform muted green, extremely subtle gentle color variation, faint tiny moss texture. NO flowers, NO strong highlights, NO dark shadows, NO recognizable object at center or corners. Seamless on all four edges so repeated tiles show no obvious pattern.
Row 2, columns 1-4: very soft grass-to-bare-earth edge transitions in order N, S, W, E. Edges must be gentle and low-contrast, blending naturally rather than forming hard bands.
Row 3, columns 1-4: soft outer corner transitions in order NW, NE, SW, SE. Gentle low-contrast grass-to-bare-earth corners with natural edge breakup.
Row 4, columns 1-4: quiet low-contrast accent variants in order: a few tiny muted clover leaves, a small flat muted pebble, a faint bare-dirt patch, a small moss patch. Accents must be tiny, calm, sparse, and not centered as bold objects.

NEGATIVE REQUIREMENTS:
- No busy grass, many flowers, strong yellow highlights, black shadows, checkerboard texture, visible tile border, repeated motif, high contrast, directional strokes, isometric angle, perspective, character, creature, bird, companion, samurai armor, kabuto helmet, ninja outfit, two-sword daisho, gun, modern object, horror mood, dark gloomy palette, letter emblem, text, watermark, UI.
- Avoid any Mario/Nintendo/Zelda/Final Fantasy/Pokemon/Dragon Quest/Secret of Mana/Okami/Sakuna characters, logos, costumes, symbols, or likenesses.

The final result must read as one cohesive 64x64 pixel-art grass autotile sheet, 16 usable seamless 16x16 tiles, quiet and map-supportive.
